rule_metadata.author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
rule_metadata.title: SMBexec.py Execution

rules.rule_text:
title: SMBexec.py Execution
id: c250b516-78d8-4664-8b86-8f3c9c0b53c8
status: experimental
description: "Similar to the wmiexec.py detector logic, this detection analytic is\
  \ looking for services.exe \nspawning cmd.exe with a command line that has the following\
  \ strings: '/Q', '/c', 'echo',' > ', \n' 2>&1'. These strings are unique to the\
  \ execution of smbexec.py, which allows a semi-interactive \nshell used through\
  \ SMB. This script functions similar to psexec.py, but does not write a service\
  \ \nbinary to disk on the target machine. Part of the RedCanary 2024 Threat Detection\
  \ Report.\n"
references:
- https://redcanary.com/threat-detection-report/threats/impacket/
author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
date: 2024/03/21
tags:
- attack.s0357
- attack.execution
- attack.t1569
- attack.t1569.002
- attack.lateral_movement
- attack.t1021
- attack.t1021.003
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: \services.exe
    Image|endswith: \cmd.exe
    CommandLine|contains:
    - /Q
    - /c
    - echo
    - '^> '
    - ' 2^>^&1 > '
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
- Unknown
level: low
